title: Audio Gumbo x Slow Drag — Monthly Dance Party
description: A monthly dance party celebrating Black culture through 70s slow jams and deep cuts. Intimate, curated vibe — exactly the kind of date where you end up closer than you planned. Atlanta-based event, tickets usually $20–$35. Arrive ready to dance (and hold each other). Doors open at 9 PM, goes until 2 AM. This month's edition is Sat May 16.
location: Atlanta — see audio-gumbo.com for exact venue and ticket link
vibe: Romantic, groovy, Black intimacy energy — slow jams meet good company

title: Elevated Food & Wine Festival (ATL) at Atlantucky Brewing
description: 4th annual food, beverage, and specialty products festival at Atlantucky Brewing. Live music, great food vendors, and a relaxed outdoor vibe. Sat May 16 only. Free to attend with purchase. Great for couples who want to graze, sip, and explore together. Arrive afternoon between 2–6 PM to enjoy the full spread.
budget: Free entry; food + drinks $15–$40 per person
vibe: Laid-back, foodie, outdoor — easy conversation and lots to explore

title: Uptown Market Atlanta — Live Music, Food & Crafts
description: Weekend market with live music, DJs, 50+ food and craft vendors, and free admission. Running every Saturday and Sunday through November. Arrive at 11 AM, grab coffee and a pastry, stroll through the vendors, catch some live tunes. Kid-friendly but perfect for a relaxed adult date too. Ends at 9 PM — ideal for an afternoon-to-early-evening outing.
budget: Free entry; food/music donations appreciated ($5–$20 suggested)
location: Uptown Market Atlanta — Sat May 16 & Sun May 17, 11 AM–9 PM
vibe: Festive, outdoor, community energy — low-key and easy-going
